rows.description: Compotus Geo. Paynter Rectoris a Secundo
rows.notes: Title at top of page. Handwriting is clear and centered; no monetary columns associated.

rows.description: Die Novembris 1712 ad Eundem diem revolutum 1713
rows.notes: Date line directly under title, clearly written and centered; no amounts.

rows.description: Imprimis reddidit comput. de remanentib: in Cesta
rows.notes: Numbers in right-hand £ s d columns align with this first entry. Pence has a small loop/"ob" style mark interpreted as halfpenny (0.5). Alignment with vertical ruling lines is clear.
Text legible; no amount appears on the corresponding right-hand columns (appears to be a listed item without its own amount).
Clear, large numerals '60 00 0' perfectly aligned with this row. All currency fields within valid ranges.
Right-hand numbers read as '63 00 9'. Pence digit is small but clearly shaped like a '9' and fits the pence column; column alignment consistent with nearby totals.
This line is present as an item but there is no visible amount in the £ s d columns (blank). Handwriting of description is legible enough to read name and 'pro triennio'.
Clear '28 00 00' aligned with this entry; the annotation 'Res. in part 28 Ann.1712' matches the left description and alignment confirms the row association.
Numerals '5 0 0' clearly visible and aligned with this line; description legible.
Right-hand shows '7 0 2' with a small loop after the pence similar to the halfpenny sign used elsewhere; read as 2½d. Slight ink shadow makes the fraction mark less crisp than others.
Numerals '5 0 0' are clear and align with this row.
Short place-name (reads as 'Rhup.' or similar). Amount '4 0 0' is clear and aligned. The place-name is slightly abbreviated but legible.
Description 'Cudlington vicar.' is legible; no corresponding numbers in the columns (appears blank).
Text 'Quit-rents' visible; no amount in the £ s d columns for this line.
Name and 'pro Triennio' visible but no amount written on the right; ink shading from shadow slightly affects legibility of the surname.
Left description is partially faded and the Latin term is abbreviated; no amount present. Interpretation of 'Capons' and 'Quadrion' is tentative due to abbreviated handwriting.
Short entry 'Ford. pro Mic.' present; no corresponding numbers. The abbreviation 'Ford.' is clear but the context is minimal.
Single-word place name 'Ealcott' (or 'Calcott') readable; no amount in columns.
Numerals '2 00 00' align with this entry and are clear.
Reads as 'Mountacule' / 'Mountacute' with 'pro Novem anni & dimid' (nine years and a half). Amount '4 15 0' is clearly written and aligns with the row.
Amount '2 8 0' is clear. The left text reads 'Hughes. pro Sex annis' (sex = six), the handwriting is legible.
Amount '2 0 0' clear and aligned; description legible.
Numbers '1 10 0' are clear. Description 'Walker pro biennio' readable and aligns with the amount.
Entry reads 'Fraklyn/pro anno' (variant spelling); '1 0 0' is clearly visible to the right.

rows.description: Minne pro Sex annis. Res in part 2
rows.notes: Amount '2 0 0' visible and aligned. Left annotation 'Res in part 2' present and legible.
Amount '0 10 0' is clear and fits the small-value entry. 'Ladden-hall' reading is confident though spelling may vary.
Right-hand reads approximately '4 17 7' with a small halfpenny loop after the pence; the pence area is slightly smudged so fraction interpreted as 1/2d. The shillings and pounds digits are clear.
Amount appears as '2 1 11' and aligns with this row. 'Tintinhull Napper' is legible though uncommon; 'pro Mic.' visible.
Amount '4 18 4' is clear and aligned. The left description reads as Hopkins with reference to a five-year term and half (quine+dimid).

Numerals appear as '4 0 10' with a small dot/loop after the pence that could be a halfpenny mark; the fractional mark is faint. The exact fraction is uncertain, chosen as 0.5 because of a small loop similar to other halfpennies on the page.

Right-hand cluster near the bottom shows '7 19 2' adjacent to this entry. The pence column has two small strokes making the digit slightly ambiguous, but '2d' is the most consistent reading and fits currency rules.
The place-name looks like 'Browhouse' (handwriting abbreviated). The immediate right-hand columns are faint here; there may be an amount but it's not legible. Treated as no visible amount.
Left text reads as 'Grethwin' or similar and indicates 'pro triennio'; the corresponding amount area is either blank or too faint to read.
Amount '4 14 11' is clearly visible near the lower right and aligns with the 'Merton. vicarage' entry above the last line. Handwriting of description is readable.
Final left-hand line reads 'Cudlington Mills pro mich:' but no amount is visible at the far right; the bottom margin shadow partially obscures this area.
Bottom-underlined aggregate reads '219 16 6' clearly. This is a calculated total line (Summa) and aligns with the vertical ruling. Very clear numerals.
